sections.heading: Background
sections.content_instructions:
Describe the current state of the UPI ecosystem relevant to this feature. Cover: (i) what exists today, (ii) the gap or limitation that motivated this change, (iii) the rationale for the proposed technical approach. Minimum 2 substantive paragraphs.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a fixed API sequence: the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI requests authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns authentication, and finally the payer PSP confirms the transaction. The amount is entered manually by the user and is validated against static limits (e.g., >₹15,000 requires PIN, >₹500 for UPI‑Lite offline). No UI‑level suggestion of amounts exists in the protocol.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual entry of the transaction amount leads to longer payment times and higher probability of typographical errors. Payers also lack quick access to historically frequent amounts, which can cause friction especially for repeat contacts or merchants.
RULE: Use ONLY these API names, field names, error codes. Never invent HTTP codes.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Write current state first, then limitations, then rationale in separate paragraphs. Keep the language engineering-level but not implementation-specific.

sections.heading: Product Construct
sections.content_instructions:
Describe the overall system design and operating model for the feature. Include: participant roles, interaction topology, key design decisions. Minimum 2 paragraphs.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount and VPA details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetails",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction after authentication"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer account/voucher as per approved request"
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails
  Step 4: Payer PSP — sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI
  Step 5: NPCI — processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction for standard UPI (PIN required for >15,000); INR 500 for UPI‑Lite offline transactions (PIN required for >500)
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens the PSP app, selects a contact or merchant VPA.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches recent transaction amounts for that VPA and shows up to three suggestions.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount (or edits it) and proceeds.
  Step 4: PSP app sends ReqPay with the chosen amount to NPCI.
  Step 5: NPCI follows the normal authentication and confirmation flow and returns the final status.
RULE: Use ONLY these API names, field names, error codes. Never invent HTTP codes.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):

  If the suggested amount exceeds the applicable limit, the app shall block the selection and display an error to the user.

sections.heading: iv. Note:
sections.content_instructions:
List all cross-flow technical notes, caveats, and mandatory operational rules that apply to this feature's implementation. Use a numbered list. Each note is a standalone, actionable technical statement. Minimum 4 numbered notes covering integration rules, edge cases, timeout handling, and any other implementation-critical details from the input.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with amount and VPA details"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetails",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction after authentication"
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer account/voucher as per approved request"
  }
]
Request Fields: [
  {
    "name": "amount",
    "type": "Decimal",
    "mandatory": true,
    "dLength": "15",
    "description": "Transaction amount in INR"
  },
  {
    "name": "payerVPA",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": true,
    "dLength": "35",
    "description": "Virtual Payment Address of the payer"
  },
  {
    "name": "payeeVPA",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": true,
    "dLength": "35",
    "description": "Virtual Payment Address of the payee"
  },
  {
    "name": "purposeCode",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": false,
    "dLength": "2",
    "description": "Code indicating transaction purpose (e.g., 14 for non\u2011pre\u2011approved debit)"
  },
  {
    "name": "initiationMode",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": false,
    "dLength": "2",
    "description": "Mode of initiation, e.g., 11 for standard UPI"
  }
]
Response Fields: [
  {
    "name": "txnStatus",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": true,
    "dLength": "10",
    "description": "Final status of the transaction (SUCCESS/FAILURE)"
  },
  {
    "name": "confirmationCode",
    "type": "String",
    "mandatory": true,
    "dLength": "20",
    "description": "Reference returned by NPCI for reconciliation"
  }
]
Error Codes (UPI format): []
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction for standard UPI (PIN required for >15,000); INR 500 for UPI‑Lite offline transactions (PIN required for >500)
Flow Sequence: [
  "Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with amount, payerVPA, payeeVPA, purposeCode",
  "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP",
  "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns RespAuthDetails",
  "Step 4: Payer PSP \u2014 sends ReqTxnConfirmation (or ReqDebit for voucher) to NPCI",
  "Step 5: NPCI \u2014 processes debit, returns RespTxnConfirmation to Payer PSP"
]
RULE: Use ONLY these API names, field names, error codes. Never invent HTTP codes.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
